summary: Implemented a large first pass of `src/melee/if/ifstock.c` for stock/coin HUD interface logic, added internal static state structs in `ifstock.static.h`, tightened `ifstock.h` prototypes from `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` to typed HSD/GX signatures, and corrected two game-mode prototypes needed by the work. The decomp.dev report showed 18 newly matched `ifstock` functions and 10 partially improved functions, increasing matched code by 4312 bytes (+0.11%).

key_files.reason: Main implementation changed from effectively empty to 927 lines of `ifStock` HUD logic, callbacks, object creation/destruction, animation requests, stock/coin display handling, and setup/teardown routines.
New internal header defining `IfStockUserData`, `GET_IFSTOCK`, global/static BSS structs such as `ifStock_804A1378`, `ifStock_804A1774`, `ifStock_804A1A8C`, and `ifStock_804A1ACC`.
Converted many `ifStock` declarations from unknown placeholders to typed prototypes using `HSD_GObj*`, `GXColor*`, `float`, `int`, and `void`.
Corrected external prototypes used by `ifstock`: `gm_80168BF8` now returns `float`, and `gm_8016A944` now returns `bool`.
Updated the definition of `gm_80168BF8` from `void` to `float`, aligning the implementation with texture animation frame usage in `ifstock`.

what_changed: Added concrete C for many `ifStock` routines covering stock display updates, coin display updates, HUD render callbacks, GObj/JObj creation, cleanup, color application, and initialization.
Introduced typed HUD user data via `struct IfStockUserData` with `player` and `mode` fields plus a `GET_IFSTOCK(gobj)` helper macro.
Mode-dispatched `fn_802F9410` now routes per-frame updates to stock, simple animation, or coin handlers based on `IfStockUserData::mode`.
Added internal state layouts for per-player stock HUD data, 130-entry character/stage icon arrays, and 16-entry ground-related HUD objects.

Replaced many `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations in `ifstock.h` with typed signatures, including render callbacks of the form `void fn(...)(HSD_GObj*, int)` and factory functions returning `HSD_GObj*`.
Corrected `gm_80168BF8` to return `float`, which is passed into `HSD_TObjReqAnimAll` for player/costume-dependent texture animation selection.
Corrected `gm_8016A944` to return `bool` in the header.

The bot report recorded 18 fully matched new `ifstock` functions including `ifStock_802FA2D0`, `fn_802F916C`, `ifStock_802F96D0`, `ifStock_802FA118`, `ifStock_802FB4EC`, `fn_802F9410`, and several small render/cleanup callbacks.

The bot report recorded 10 additional `ifstock` functions improved but not fully matched, including `ifStock_802F98E8`, `ifStock_802F8298`, `ifStock_802FAEC4`, `fn_802FA8C0`, `fn_802F8E08`, and `ifStock_802F7EFC`.
smart_moves: Typed prototypes in `ifstock.h` reduced unknown-call ambiguity and made callsites reveal expected argument/return behavior, especially for HSD callback signatures.
Adding `ifstock.static.h` kept uncertain internal layout work local to the module while still giving enough structure to compile and match many routines.
Changing `gm_80168BF8` to `float` was important because `ifstock` uses it directly as an animation frame argument to `HSD_TObjReqAnimAll`; leaving it as `void` would obscure a real data dependency.
The `GET_IFSTOCK` macro encoded the repeated cast from GObj user data to `IfStockUserData`, making callback decomp cleaner and more consistent.
The implementation preserved address-based names and offset-style field names where semantics were still uncertain, avoiding overnaming unsupported by evidence.
Using small inline helpers such as `fn_802F9410_inline` and `copyColor` localized repeated animation/color patterns without forcing premature public symbols.
The PR used matching-control constructs visible in the diff, including `PAD_STACK`, `#pragma dont_inline`, and explicit static inline helpers, to coax code generation while keeping readable C.
decomp_lessons: For HUD/interface modules, first recovering object/user-data lifetimes and callback signatures can unlock many small matches even when full struct field names remain unknown.
When a function is used as an animation selector, validate its return type from callsite behavior; `void` placeholders can hide required float return values.
Static module headers are a useful staging area for inferred BSS layouts and private structs before names and field meanings are mature enough for wider headers.
Do not block progress waiting for perfect struct names: address-based global names and offset-style fields can still produce substantial matching gains while preserving uncertainty.
Render callbacks commonly take `(HSD_GObj*, int renderpass)` and gate `HSD_GObj_JObjCallback` on HUD visibility flags; this pattern is reusable in other `if` HUD files.
Stock/coin HUD code frequently combines `HSD_JObjReqAnimAll`, `HSD_TObjReqAnimAll`, `HSD_AObjSetRate(..., 0.0f)`, and `HSD_JObjAnimAll`; preserving this ordering appears important for matching.
Object teardown routines in this area use null checks followed by `HSD_GObjPLink_80390228` or `HSD_GObj_80390CAC`, then clear stored pointers or memory with `memzero`.
naming_conventions: Kept public module functions in address form such as `ifStock_802F8298`, `ifStock_802FAEC4`, and `ifStock_802FB650` when no semantic name was established.
Used `fn_` prefix for unnamed local/internal functions such as `fn_802F9410`, `fn_802FAC34`, and render callbacks.
Named private globals by module plus address, e.g. `ifStock_804A1378`, `ifStock_804A1774`, `ifStock_804A1A8C`, and `ifStock_804A1ACC`.
Used offset-style struct fields (`x0`, `x4`, `x204`, `x10C`, `x108`, etc.) where field semantics were not confidently known.
Introduced semantic minimal names only where directly supported, such as `IfStockUserData`, `player`, `mode`, `stocks`, `coins`, and `GET_IFSTOCK`.
Temporary vector locals followed simple `vecA`, `vecB`, `vecC` naming rather than inventing positional semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK(8)` and `PAD_STACK(0x10)` were used in functions where stack shape mattered.
`#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` were used around `ifStock_802FAEC4`, suggesting deliberate control of inlining/codegen.
A `static inline` helper, `fn_802F9410_inline`, was used for a repeated animation sequence inside the mode dispatcher.
Another `static inline` helper, `copyColor`, wrote `GXColor` components into `HSD_MObj` material diffuse fields for a compact matched color-copy routine.
Explicit float constants such as `0.0f`, `10.0f`, `2.45f`, `-21.0f`, `11.0f`, and `10000.0f` were preserved in HUD positioning and visibility logic.
The code uses direct JObj/DObj/MObj/TObj access chains such as `jobj2->u.dobj->mobj->tobj` to match texture animation and material operations.

The implementation keeps suspicious or partially understood expressions in offset form, including comments like `// .x10[i]` and `// not inlined`, rather than disguising them with speculative abstraction.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code for GALE01 increased to 42.93%, +0.11%, +4312 bytes.
Reported 10 `ifstock` functions improved but still unmatched, with percentages ranging from 18.30% for `ifStock_802F7EFC` to 95.28% for `ifStock_802F9F48`.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for replacements of `ifStock_804A1378` offset fields with semantic names.
Search for subsequent work that fully matches `ifStock_802F98E8`, `ifStock_802F8298`, `ifStock_802FAEC4`, `fn_802FA8C0`, and `fn_802F8E08`.
Check whether `IfStockUserData` and `ifStock_804A1378_x204` were eventually merged as the TODO suggests.
Look for future PRs that rename `fn_802F9410` or identify the semantics of modes 0, 1, and 2.
Search for additional `gm_1601` prototype corrections around animation frame/color selection helpers.
